message:
perf+a11y(wills-estate): Lighthouse/CWV fixes — loading skeletons, heading order, ARIA roles, OG image paths (hgjp.29)

- Add VizSkeleton loading state to all 5 dynamic viz imports (CLS prevention)
- Fix TOC heading order: h3→p to avoid h1→h3 skip (Lighthouse heading-order)
- Fix TierTriageViz container: role="list"→role="group" (aria-required-children)
- Add aria-hidden to remaining decorative ArrowRight icons
- Fix OG/twitter image asset paths (4 levels → 3 levels up, was breaking build)

Lighthouse after: perf 72, a11y 97, best-practices 96, SEO 69 (draft noindex).
CLS 0, remaining gaps are site-wide nav contrast + localhost LCP overhead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Build passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1db.6","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1db","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T02:27:12.627362067Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1lj","title":"Design and build TldrHero component","description":"# Task: Design and Build TldrHero Component ## Overview Create the hero section for the /tldr page - the first thing users see. Must immediately communicate what this page is about and draw them in. ## Content ### Headline "The Agentic Coding Flywheel" or similar - Compelling, clear, memorable - Matches site's confident tone ### Subheadline Brief explanation of the flywheel concept: "Seven tools that work together to supercharge multi-agent coding workflows. Each tool makes the others more powerful." ### Visual Element Either: - Animated flywheel graphic (spinning gear/wheel with tool icons) - Mini version of synergy diagram - Abstract particles/motion suggesting interconnection - Tool icons orbiting a center point ### Call-to-Action - "Explore the tools" → smooth scroll to tool grid - "View synergies" → scroll to synergy diagram ## Visual Design - Full-width, generous height (50-70vh) - Background gradient or subtle pattern - Large, bold typography - Centered layout - Tool icons floating/orbiting as decoration ## Animation - Headline entrance: fade up - Subheadline: fade up with delay - Visual element: continuous subtle animation - CTA buttons: appear last - Parallax scroll effect (subtle) ## Component Structure ```tsx // Gradient + decorations ``` ## Props Interface ```typescript interface TldrHeroProps { title?: string; subtitle?: string; onScrollToTools?: () => void; className?: string; } ``` ## Implementation Notes - Follow existing Hero component pattern - Use Framer Motion for animations - useInView for triggering animations - Smooth scroll behavior ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Visually striking first impression - [ ] Communicates page purpose immediately - [ ] Animations are polished - [ ] CTA scrolls smoothly to content - [ ] Responsive design - [ ] Reduced motion support","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:15:56.427136450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T19:46:05.018158162Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T19:46:05.018158162Z","close_reason":"Completed as part of TLDR page implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1uxr","title":"AntiPatternCardsViz click still fails to pin hover-previewed card","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:12.938075748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:21.070456559Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1uxr","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5h5z","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:12.938075748Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-21e","title":"Add repo_updater to content.ts (28 stars)","description":"## What Add repo_updater project to the projects array in jeffreyemanuel-site/lib/content.ts ## Project Details - **Name**: repo_updater (or "Repo Updater") - **GitHub**: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/repo_updater - **Stars**: 28 - **Description**: "A beautiful, automation-friendly CLI for synchronizing GitHub repositories. Keep dozens of repos in sync with a single command." - **User specifically mentioned this repo** ## Background & Context - CLI tool for managing multiple GitHub repositories - Designed for users with many repos (like Jeffrey with 70+ repos) - "Automation-friendly" suggests JSON output and scriptability - Part of developer workflow tooling - Research at /data/projects/repo_updater/ ## Implementation ```typescript { name: "Repo Updater", kind: "oss", badge: "28 stars", href: "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/repo_updater", short: "Beautiful CLI for keeping dozens of GitHub repos in sync.", description: "A polished, automation-friendly tool for synchronizing multiple GitHub repositories. Ideal for developers managing large collections of repos who need batch updates, status checks, and coordination across projects.", tags: ["CLI", "GitHub", "DevTools", "Automation", "Rust"], // verify language } ``` ## Considerations - Emphasize the "beautiful" / polished nature per description - Highlight automation-friendly aspects - May be written in Rust (common for Jeffrey's CLIs) - verify ## Acceptance Criteria - Project appears in projects array - Description highlights both UX polish and automation features - Tags accurately reflect technology","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T15:25:08.925931093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:14:20.534507743Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T16:14:20.534507743Z","close_reason":"Added Repo Updater to content.ts with 28 stars badge","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-24j","title":"Research NTM (Named Tmux Manager) source code","description":"# Task: Research NTM (Named Tmux Manager) Source Code ## Purpose Review the NTM source code to extract accurate information for the /tldr page. ## Source Location - Path: /data/projects/ntm - Language: Go - GitHub: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm ## Research Questions ### Functionality 1. What exactly does NTM do? (Read main.go, cmd/*) 2. How does it interact with tmux? (What tmux commands?) 3. What agent types does it support? (Claude, Codex, Gemini?) 4. How does broadcasting work? 5. What capture/output features exist? ### Implementation 1. What Go libraries are used? (Bubble Tea confirmed?) 2. How is the TUI implemented? 3. What's the architecture? (Commands, services, etc.) 4. Performance characteristics? 5. Binary size, dependencies? ### Integrations 1. Does it import/integrate with Agent Mail? 2. Does it integrate with SLB? 3. Does it work with CASS? 4. How are hooks configured? ## Deliverable Structured summary in this format: ```markdown ## NTM (Named Tmux Manager) ### What It Does [2-3 sentences based on actual code] ### Why It's Useful [2-3 sentences - value prop] ### Implementation Highlights - Language: Go - Key tech: [specific libraries] - Performance: [specific metrics if available] - Binary: [single file? size?] ### Synergies - Agent Mail: [specific integration found in code] - SLB: [specific integration] - CASS: [specific integration] ``` ## Execution Use subagent with Explore capability to review source code systematically. Focus on: README.md, main.go, cmd/*, any integration files.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:16:40.418690786Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:47.385353761Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:47.385288510Z","close_reason":"Content for this tool already exists in lib/content.ts with comprehensive whatItDoes, whyItsUseful, implementationHighlights, synergies, techStack, keyFeatures, and useCases.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-27n","title":"Build GitHub activity API integration","description":"## Overview Extend the existing lib/github-stats.ts to include activity data for the heartbeat widget. ## IMPORTANT: File Location **Extend lib/github-stats.ts** - Per AGENTS.md, do not create new files when existing files can be extended. The github-stats.ts file already has the infrastructure we need. ## Current lib/github-stats.ts Already Has - fetchGitHubStats() returning totalStars, repoCount, followers, fetchedAt - GitHub API integration with token support - Caching with revalidate - Error handling with fallback values - formatNumber() utility ## Data to ADD to Existing Interface ```typescript // Extend GitHubStats interface in lib/github-stats.ts export interface GitHubStats { totalStars: number; repoCount: number; followers: number; fetchedAt: number; // NEW fields: latestCommitAt?: string; // ISO timestamp of most recent commit starsThisWeek?: number; // Stars gained in last 7 days (if feasible) isActive: boolean; // true if commit in last 7 days } ``` ## Implementation Approach 1. Modify existing fetchGitHubStats() function 2. Add a secondary API call to get recent events/commits 3. Use GitHub Events API: GET /users/{username}/events 4. Parse for PushEvent to find latest commit ## Caching Strategy (already in place) - Current: revalidate: 3600 (1 hour) - keep this - Fallback to stale data on API failure - already implemented - Extend fallback values for new fields ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] GitHubStats interface extended with new fields - [ ] fetchGitHubStats() returns activity data - [ ] No new files created - all changes in lib/github-stats.ts - [ ] Fallback values provided for new fields - [ ] Rate limiting respected","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:09:44.877297254Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T08:05:51.711827138Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T08:05:51.711827138Z","close_reason":"Implemented in commit b1cb57c: Two Worlds section with parallax effects, skill transfer cards; GitHub Heartbeat widget with live activity feed, animated heartbeat visualization, streak badges","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} @@ -80,6 +81,7 @@ {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5io","title":"Feature: Mobile bottom sheet for tool details","description":"# Mobile Bottom Sheet for Tool Details ## Overview Replace inline card expansion with a bottom sheet modal on mobile devices. This provides a better mobile UX by not pushing content around and giving more room for details. ## Problem with Current Approach - Expanding a card pushes all content below it down - User loses context of where they were in the grid - Expanded content is cramped in card width - Difficult to compare tools (can only expand one at a time) ## Proposed Solution On mobile (< 768px), clicking "Show More" opens a bottom sheet that: - Slides up from bottom - Contains full tool details - Has close button and swipe-to-dismiss - Leaves card grid visible but dimmed behind ## Design Specifications ### Bottom Sheet Anatomy ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ (Dimmed backdrop) │ │ │ ├─────────────────────────────────────┤ ← Drag handle │ ═══════ │ │ [Icon] Tool Name [✕ Close] │ │ Full tool name • ★ 1.4K │ ├─────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ What It Does │ │ [Full description text] │ │ │ │ Why It's Useful │ │ [Full explanation] │ │ │ │ Key Features │ │ • Feature 1 │ │ • Feature 2 │ │ │ │ [View on GitHub →] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Interaction States - **Opening**: Slides up with spring physics - **Open**: ~80% viewport height, scrollable content - **Dismissing**: Swipe down past threshold or tap backdrop - **Closed**: Slides down, backdrop fades ## Technical Implementation ### Using Framer Motion ```tsx function ToolBottomSheet({ tool, isOpen, onClose }) { return ( {isOpen && ( <> {/* Backdrop */} {/* Sheet */} { if (info.offset.y > 100 || info.velocity.y > 500) { onClose(); } }} className="fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0 z-50 max-h-[85vh] rounded-t-3xl bg-slate-900 overflow-hidden" > {/* Drag handle */}
{/* Content */}
{/* Tool details here */}
)} ); } ``` ### Responsive Integration ```tsx // In TldrToolCard const isMobile = useMediaQuery('(max-width: 767px)'); const [sheetOpen, setSheetOpen] = useState(false); // Different behavior based on viewport const handleExpand = () => { if (isMobile) { setSheetOpen(true); } else { onToggleExpand?.(); // Original inline expansion } }; ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - `components/tool-bottom-sheet.tsx` (new) - Bottom sheet component - `components/tldr-tool-card.tsx` - Conditional expansion behavior - `hooks/use-media-query.ts` (if not exists) - Media query hook ## Accessibility - Focus trap when sheet is open - Escape key closes sheet - `aria-modal="true"` on sheet - Return focus to trigger button on close - Backdrop click closes (with visual indication) ## Performance - Lazy load sheet content (only render when opening) - Use `will-change: transform` for smooth animation - Portal to document.body to escape stacking contexts ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows bottom sheet on mobile when expanding tool - [ ] Desktop retains inline expansion behavior - [ ] Sheet slides up with spring animation - [ ] Swipe down to dismiss works - [ ] Backdrop tap closes sheet - [ ] Focus trapped inside sheet - [ ] Escape key closes - [ ] Content scrollable within sheet - [ ] GitHub link accessible in sheet","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-12T22:52:25.875281504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:22:26.460230109Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:22:26.460151013Z","close_reason":"Integrated mobile bottom sheet into TldrToolGrid. On mobile ( = {}; for (const { id, repo } of TOOL_REPOS) { const [owner, name] = repo.split('/'); const { data } = await octokit.repos.get({ owner, repo: name }); stars[id] = data.stargazers_count; console.log(`[STARS] ${id}: ${data.stargazers_count}`); } return stars; } // Write to lib/data/tldr-stars.json ``` ### Update tldr-data.ts ```typescript import starsData from './data/tldr-stars.json'; export const tldrTools: TldrTool[] = [ { id: 'ntm', stars: starsData.ntm ?? 0, // ... } ]; ``` ### Build script integration ```json { "scripts": { "prebuild": "bun run scripts/fetch-tool-stars.ts", "build": "next build" } } ``` ## Fallback Handling ```typescript // If stars data is missing or stale, use hardcoded fallbacks const DEFAULT_STARS: Record = { 'ntm': 69, 'agent-mail': 1400, // ... from content.ts }; export const getToolStars = (toolId: string): number => { return starsData[toolId] ?? DEFAULT_STARS[toolId] ?? 0; }; ``` ## Caching Strategy - Stars cached in JSON file - Updated on each build - Fallback to hardcoded values if fetch fails - No runtime API calls ## Error Handling ```typescript async function fetchStarsSafely() { try { return await fetchAllStars(); } catch (error) { console.warn('[STARS] Failed to fetch, using cached values'); return existingStarsData; } } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 13 tools have star counts - [ ] Stars update on each deploy - [ ] Build doesn't fail if GitHub API unavailable - [ ] Fallback values reasonable","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:28:10.687009880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:17:44.646674015Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:17:44.646576424Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5s9","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-djw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:33Z","created_by":"import"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5si","title":"Add animated data flow between flywheel nodes","description":"# Task: Animated Data Flow ## Objective Add visible animation showing how data/messages flow between tools in the flywheel. ## Current Behavior - Static lines connect tools - Hover shows connection exists ## Enhanced Behavior - Lines have subtle, continuous animation suggesting activity - On hover, the lines TO that tool pulse more prominently - Direction of flow is visible (arrows or particle direction) ## Animation Options ### Option A: Pulsing Lines - Lines have a gradient that slowly moves along the path - Simple, low-performance-cost - Shows directionality through gradient direction ### Option B: Particle Flow - Small dots travel along connection lines - More visually striking but higher perf cost - Clear directionality ### Option C: Glow Pulse - Connections glow briefly in sequence - Shows the "ripple" of data through the system - Medium complexity ## Recommended: Option A (Pulsing Gradient) - Best balance of visual impact and performance - Works well with prefers-reduced-motion (can be static) - Enhances existing design without overwhelming ## Implementation Notes - Use CSS animations or Framer Motion - Consider SVG stroke-dashoffset animation for gradient effect - Test on mobile for performance ## Files Affected - components/flywheel-visualization.tsx ## Dependencies - None (can work on existing component)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:04:47.673270271Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:23:01.668327319Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:23:01.668327319Z","close_reason":"Implemented continuous pulsing dash animation on flywheel connection lines using stroke-dasharray/dashoffset. Animation respects prefers-reduced-motion and speeds up when highlighted.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5sjq","title":"Wills article smoke test should fail if a visualization stays on the loading skeleton","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:49.016168703Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:49.016168703Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5sjq","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:49.016168703Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w4","title":"Design GitHub heartbeat visual component","description":"## Overview Create the visual 'heartbeat' component that shows live GitHub activity status. ## Visual Concept Options 1. **Pulsing Dot**: Green dot that pulses when recently active 2. **Mini Contribution Graph**: Compact heatmap of last 7 days 3. **Activity Ticker**: Scrolling recent commits 4. **Status Badge**: 'Last commit: 2 hours ago' Recommend: Combination - Pulsing status dot + compact stats. ## Component Design ```typescript interface GitHubHeartbeatProps { activity: GitHubActivity; variant: 'minimal' | 'detailed' | 'full'; showLastCommit?: boolean; linkToProfile?: boolean; } ``` ## Visual States 1. **Active** (commit in last 24h): Green pulse 2. **Recent** (commit in last 7 days): Amber glow 3. **Idle** (no recent activity): Gray 4. **Loading**: Skeleton/shimmer 5. **Error**: Graceful fallback to static ## Animation - Subtle pulse animation for active state - Smooth transitions between states - Respects prefers-reduced-motion ## Integration - Can be placed in hero, stats grid, or footer - Should be small and non-intrusive - Links to GitHub profile on click ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All visual states implemented - [ ] Animations are smooth and subtle - [ ] Accessible (status conveyed to screen readers) - [ ] Works at different sizes/variants","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:09:46.220855480Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T08:05:51.710050300Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T08:05:51.710050300Z","close_reason":"Implemented in commit b1cb57c: Two Worlds section with parallax effects, skill transfer cards; GitHub Heartbeat widget with live activity feed, animated heartbeat visualization, streak badges","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","title":"FEATURE: Design & Build UI Components for /tldr Page","description":"# FEATURE: Design & Build UI Components for /tldr Page ## Purpose Create the reusable React components needed for the /tldr page. These components must meet the highest UI/UX standards - this is a showcase page that demonstrates technical excellence. ## Components Required ### 1. TldrToolCard Component The main card component for displaying each tool. Must include: - Tool icon (from Lucide or custom) - Tool name and short tagline - Expandable/collapsible sections for: - "What it does" (always visible or prominent) - "Why it's useful" - "Implementation" (language, key tech) - "Synergies" (connected tools) - Visual indicator of "primary" vs "supporting" tool status - GitHub stars badge - Link to repo - Smooth hover states and transitions ### 2. TldrToolGrid Component Layout component that: - Arranges tool cards in responsive grid - Groups by category (Primary Flywheel / Supporting Tools) - Handles staggered entrance animations - Maintains visual hierarchy ### 3. TldrSynergyDiagram Component Interactive visualization showing: - All tools as nodes - Connections between synergistic tools - Hover to highlight connections - Click to filter/focus - Animated flow lines showing data/communication paths - Should be visually striking but not overwhelming ### 4. TldrHero Component Page header with: - Compelling headline about the flywheel concept - Brief intro paragraph - Visual element (maybe animated flywheel graphic) - Smooth scroll to tools section ## Design Principles - Consistent with existing site design language - Dark theme with slate colors, gradients - Accent colors per tool category (emerald for primary, violet for supporting, etc.) - Generous whitespace - Typography hierarchy - Microinteractions on every interactive element ## Animation Requirements - Entrance animations (staggered, spring physics) - Hover states (scale, glow, shadow changes) - Section expand/collapse (smooth height transitions) - Synergy diagram (flowing particles on connections) - Respect prefers-reduced-motion ## Accessibility - Keyboard navigable - ARIA labels on interactive elements - Focus indicators - Screen reader friendly ## Technical Notes - Use Framer Motion for animations - Follow existing component patterns (see endorsement-card.tsx, project-card.tsx) - Memoize where appropriate - Lazy load heavy components","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:13:32.193901182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T19:46:13.036333859Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T19:46:13.036333859Z","close_reason":"Completed - TLDR page fully implemented with all components and navigation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1lj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:30:06.680974651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-8uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:30:06.733973428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-gy1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:30:06.630736904Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-uv7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:30:06.608590650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-uw1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:30:06.655937231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-wsp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:30:06.706844238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5xk","title":"Fix RSS feed sort stability","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-17T03:21:56.600621099Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T03:22:33.078654484Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T03:22:33.078654484Z","close_reason":"Fixed unstable sort in RSS feed by handling NaN dates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} @@ -93,6 +95,7 @@ {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-7brk","title":"Separate and Configure Test Runners","description":"Configure 'vitest.config.ts' to explicitly exclude E2E tests to prevent runner collision. Update 'package.json' scripts to clearly distinguish between 'test:unit' (Vitest) and 'test:e2e' (Playwright). Ensure 'bun test' runs only unit tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T18:04:23.799387620Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T18:07:45.215665804Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T18:07:45.215665804Z","close_reason":"Updated vitest.config.ts to exclude e2e tests and updated package.json scripts. Verified that 'bun run test' (Vitest) respects the config, while 'bun test' (Bun native) requires separate arguments if used.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-7brk","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-pvmw","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-15T18:04:23.880298906Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-7c1","title":"Task: Create reusable BottomSheet component","description":"# Create Reusable BottomSheet Component ## Overview Create a well-designed, reusable BottomSheet component for mobile UX that can be used across the site. ## Why a Separate Component - Used by mobile tool details feature (5io) - May be reused for mobile navigation, filters, etc. - Encapsulates complex gesture handling and accessibility - Standardizes mobile modal patterns across the site ## Component API ### File: `components/ui/bottom-sheet.tsx` ```typescript export interface BottomSheetProps { /** Whether the sheet is open */ isOpen: boolean; /** Callback when sheet should close */ onClose: () => void; /** Sheet content */ children: React.ReactNode; /** Optional title for the sheet header */ title?: string; /** Maximum height as percentage of viewport (default: 85) */ maxHeight?: number; /** Whether to show drag handle (default: true) */ showDragHandle?: boolean; /** Whether to close on backdrop click (default: true) */ closeOnBackdrop?: boolean; /** Whether to close on Escape key (default: true) */ closeOnEscape?: boolean; /** Callback when open animation completes */ onOpenComplete?: () => void; /** Additional class for the sheet content area */ contentClassName?: string; } ``` ## Implementation ```typescript "use client"; import { useRef, useEffect, useCallback } from "react"; import { motion, AnimatePresence, useDragControls, PanInfo } from "framer-motion"; import { X } from "lucide-react"; import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"; import { useReducedMotion } from "framer-motion"; export function BottomSheet({ isOpen, onClose, children, title, maxHeight = 85, showDragHandle = true, closeOnBackdrop = true, closeOnEscape = true, onOpenComplete, contentClassName, }: BottomSheetProps) { const sheetRef = useRef(null); Build Analysis Run production build with bundle analyzer to generate size report: ```bash npm run build 2>&1 | grep -E "(Route|chunk|KB)" ``` ### 2. Key Findings #### Three.js Chunk - **Size:** ~854 KB (gzipped) - **Status:** ✅ Already dynamically imported - **Location:** components/three-scene.tsx - **Import Pattern:** ```tsx const HeroScene = dynamic(() => import("@/components/three-scene"), { ssr: false, }); ``` - **Impact:** Does not affect initial page load; loaded on-demand #### Command Palette - **Size:** ~17 KB - **Status:** ✅ Already dynamically imported - **Location:** components/client-shell.tsx line 17 - **Import Pattern:** ```tsx const CommandPalette = dynamic(() => import("@/components/command-palette"), { ssr: false, }); ``` - **Impact:** Loaded only when user opens Cmd+K #### Keyboard Shortcuts Modal - **Size:** ~5 KB - **Status:** ✅ Already dynamically imported - **Location:** components/client-shell.tsx line 20 - **Import Pattern:** ```tsx const KeyboardShortcutsModal = dynamic( () => import("@/components/keyboard-shortcuts-modal"), { ssr: false } ); ``` ### 3. Framework Dependencies | Dependency | Size | Notes | |------------|------|-------| | React 19 | ~45 KB | Core, required | | Framer Motion | ~85 KB | Animation engine | | Fuse.js | ~12 KB | Fuzzy search | | Three.js | ~854 KB | 3D scene (lazy) | ### 4. Recommendations 1. ✅ Three.js already lazy-loaded - no action needed 2. ✅ Modals already lazy-loaded - no action needed 3. Consider lazy-loading Fuse.js if search is rarely used (low priority) ## Conclusion Bundle optimization is already well-implemented. The heavy dependencies (Three.js, modals) are already code-split via dynamic imports with `ssr: false`. Comprehensive Flywheel Tools Showcase","description":"# EPIC: /tldr Page - Comprehensive Flywheel Tools Showcase ## Background & Vision This epic covers the creation of a new `/tldr` page on the personal site that serves as a comprehensive, visually stunning showcase of all the Agentic Coding Flywheel tools. The page should be THE definitive quick-reference for understanding what each tool does, why it's valuable, and how they work together. ## Why This Matters The flywheel tools represent a cohesive ecosystem for multi-agent coding workflows. Currently, understanding how they fit together requires visiting multiple repos. This page will: 1. Provide instant understanding of each tool's purpose 2. Highlight the synergistic connections between tools 3. Serve as an onboarding resource for new users 4. Demonstrate the ecosystem's completeness and sophistication ## Core Tools to Feature ### PRIMARY FLYWHEEL TOOLS (7 tools): 1. **NTM (Named Tmux Manager)** - Multi-agent tmux orchestration 2. **SLB (Simultaneous Launch Button)** - Two-person rule for dangerous commands 3. **MCP Agent Mail** - Inter-agent messaging and coordination 4. **BV (Beads Viewer)** - Graph analytics for Steve Yegge's Beads system 5. **UBS (Ultimate Bug Scanner)** - Multi-language static analysis 6. **CM (CASS Memory System)** - Persistent memory across sessions 7. **CASS (Coding Agent Session Search)** - Full-text search across sessions ### SUPPORTING TOOLS (5+ tools): 8. **DCG (Destructive Command Guard)** - Safety hook for blocking dangerous commands 9. **GIIL** - Git-backed interactive issue list 10. **XF** - Fast cross-file search and replace 11. **Source to Prompt TUI** - Convert codebases to LLM prompts 12. **Repo Updater** - Sync multiple GitHub repos 13. **Agentic Coding Flywheel Setup** - One-command environment setup ## Key Synergies to Highlight - Beads (Steve Yegge's) → BV provides visualization/analytics - Agent Mail ↔ Beads: Task IDs link conversations to issues - NTM → Agent Mail: Spawned agents communicate via mail - SLB → Agent Mail: Approval requests delivered to inboxes - UBS → BV: Scanner results create blocking issues - CASS → CM: Semantic search over stored memories - DCG → NTM/Claude Code: Safety layer for all agent commands ## UI/UX Requirements - **Extremely polished** - This is a showcase page - **Microinteractions** - Hover states, smooth transitions - **Visual hierarchy** - Primary tools prominent, supporting tools organized - **Synergy visualization** - Show connections between tools - **Responsive** - Works beautifully on all screen sizes - **Accessible** - Keyboard navigation, screen reader support ## Page Structure (per tool) Each tool card should have these sections: 1. **What it does** - Clear, concise explanation (2-3 sentences) 2. **Why it's useful** - The value proposition (2-3 sentences) 3. **Implementation** - Language, key tech highlights (very concise) 4. **Synergies** - Which tools it connects to and how (concise) ## Technical Approach 1. Create new route: app/tldr/page.tsx and layout.tsx 2. Build reusable TldrToolCard component 3. Build TldrSynergyDiagram component 4. Add animations via Framer Motion 5. Use existing design system (slate colors, gradients, etc.) ## Source Code Research Required Each tool requires a subagent to review its source code and extract: - Accurate description of functionality - Key implementation details (language, patterns, performance) - Actual synergies based on code (imports, integrations) ## Success Criteria - All 12+ tools documented with accurate, compelling copy - Visual design matches or exceeds existing site quality - Synergies clearly communicated - Page loads fast (lazy loading where needed) - Mobile-responsive and accessible","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:13:13.459530629Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T19:49:15.278387502Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T19:49:15.278387502Z","close_reason":"EPIC {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","title":"CSS styles: decide on sm- reuse vs wep- namespace (globals.css)","description":"Audit `app/globals.css` for every `sm-*` class the slack article uses (sm-scope, sm-body, sm-progress-bar, sm-drop-cap, sm-section-title, sm-subheading, sm-subheading-eyebrow, sm-code-block, sm-table-wrap, sm-table, sm-toc, sm-viz-wrap, sm-callout, sm-callout-emerald, sm-insight-card, sm-sign-off, sm-decisions, sm-display-title, sm-fade-section, sm-visible, sm-reveal-ready, etc.). ## Decision Two options: - **Reuse `sm-*`**: simplest. Zero new CSS. Works out of the box if the wills article uses the same visual language. - **Namespace to `wep-*`**: safer if we expect the wills articles visual identity to diverge (e.g., warmer palette, softer shadows). Adds ~800 lines of CSS. Default: reuse. Only introduce `wep-*` if a section bead requires a visually distinct style that cannot be expressed as a modifier class (e.g., `sm-insight-card sm-insight-card--wep-warm`). ## Action - Read `components/slack-migration-article.tsx`: list every `sm-` class referenced. - Run the article through a dry build; confirm no missing classes. - If any warm-palette variants are needed (e.g., an amber-accented callout for the "if I die tomorrow" deliverable), add them as modifier classes on the existing `sm-` definitions. ## Acceptance criteria - Article renders correctly with the existing stylesheet. - No CSS duplication unless strictly required. - Any new additions documented with a brief comment at the top of the new block.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:38.052564575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:41:14.190879525Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:41:14.186701102Z","close_reason":"Audited the wills article against the shared sm-* stylesheet surface.
